diagrams.title: TCP Header Structure
diagrams.description: A 20-byte (minimum) TCP header with all fields shown in 32-bit alignment
elements: Data Offset (4 bits) | Reserved (3 bits) | Flags (9 bits)
diagrams.purpose: Shows the complete structure of a TCP segment header, enabling understanding of how flow control (Window field), reliability (Seq/Ack numbers), and error detection (Checksum) are implemented.

diagrams.title: TCP State Transition Diagram
diagrams.description: Finite state machine showing TCP connection states from LISTEN to CLOSED
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the lifecycle of a TCP connection from opening (three-way handshake) through data transfer to graceful termination (four-way handshake), helping students understand TIME_WAIT and connection states.

diagrams.title: TCP Congestion Control Timeline
diagrams.description: Graph showing congestion window (cwnd) evolution over time with slow start, congestion avoidance, and loss events
elements: Loss event at ssthresh: Sharp drop to 1 MSS (timeout) or ssthresh/2 (dup ACKs)
Labels: Slow Start, Congestion Avoidance, Fast Recovery regions
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how TCP dynamically adjusts its sending rate based on network conditions, demonstrating the adaptive nature of congestion control algorithms.
